headline: UAE MGX closes $49B AI fund - one of largest ever
detail: A $49 billion AI-focused fund signals massive sustained institutional capital flowing into AI infrastructure - chips, data centers, and the software stack on top. This directly benefits your semiconductor and tech holdings: SMH, SOXL, CRWD, and NVDA (if you add). It reinforces the bull case for the sector but also means valuations stay stretched.
rec: No immediate trade - your SMH and SOXL already capture this theme. If NVDA pulls into the scanner entry zone of $198.39-$198.62 with stop $197.35, that's a technically clean level to consider a small starter, but only if you have margin headroom; don't chase above that range.

headline: Microsoft planning thousands of layoffs amid AI spending push
detail: MSFT is cutting headcount to fund AI capex - this is a double signal: AI infrastructure demand stays high (good for chip names), but it also flags that even mega-caps are feeling cost pressure from the AI arms race. Broadly neutral-to-positive for semiconductors; mild negative read for software/SaaS if spending gets scrutinized.
rec: No action needed - CRWD is your only software-adjacent name and it's holding fine at +6.07%. This headline doesn't change the setup there; just monitor CRWD above your $590 stop.

headline: Anthropic cleared for international use ahead of IPO
detail: Regulatory green light for Anthropic's international expansion accelerates AI adoption globally, adding another demand layer for GPU compute and the chip ecosystem underneath it. Positive read-through for your chip-heavy book - more AI model deployment means more semiconductor demand. Not a direct catalyst but confirms the macro tailwind.
rec: No new trade warranted from this alone - it supports holding SMH and SOXL at current levels. Stay patient; your stops are your protection.

headline: Oil wobbles as U.S.-Iran talks break down
detail: Oil uncertainty could briefly lift energy volatility but has limited direct impact on tech and chip names - your primary sector. However, if oil spikes on geopolitical escalation, it feeds inflation fears which pressure rate-sensitive growth stocks including everything in your book.
rec: No action today - but if oil meaningfully spikes and rate fear re-enters the narrative, that's the scenario where QQQ tests toward $670 stop and your leveraged positions (SOXL, SPAL) feel it first. Keep those stops active.
